title: OAuth authentication via Codex login crate — replace API key auth
purpose: The OpenAI provider originally authenticated via an API key (SecretString in ProviderConfig). Tom's directive made OAuth the primary auth path. Codex uses OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code with PKCE against auth.openai.com and stores its own credential at ~/.codex/auth.json. Norn implements the compatible flow but owns its credential lifecycle and persists a compatible document at $NORN_HOME/auth/auth.json (default ~/.norn/auth/auth.json). This brief replaces the API key default with OAuth token management.
task: Create an AuthProvider trait abstracting token acquisition. Implement OAuthAuthProvider around Norn's Codex-compatible OAuth manager — handles login flow, Norn-owned token storage, proactive refresh, and 401 recovery. Replace ProviderConfig's api_key with an AuthSource enum (OAuth or LegacyApiKey for testing). OpenAI provider's request pipeline calls auth_provider.apply_auth() before every HTTP request. Test with mock auth provider. Remove API key as the default path.

requirements.title: AuthProvider trait and AuthSource enum
requirements.spec: An AuthProvider trait SHALL abstract how authentication headers are applied to HTTP requests. AuthSource SHALL distinguish between OAuth (primary) and legacy API key (testing only).
acceptance: AuthProvider trait in crates/norn/src/provider/auth.rs: async fn apply_auth(&self, request: reqwest::RequestBuilder) -> Result<reqwest::RequestBuilder, ProviderError>
AuthProvider trait requires Send + Sync for Arc sharing
AuthSource enum: OAuth { auth_root: Option<PathBuf> }, ApiKey { key: SecretString }
AuthSource::OAuth is the default construction path
AuthSource::ApiKey exists for env-gated integration tests only, NOT as a production path
ProviderConfig replaces api_key: SecretString with auth_source: AuthSource
Test: AuthProvider trait is object-safe (compiles as Arc<dyn AuthProvider>)

requirements.title: OAuthAuthProvider with Norn-owned storage
requirements.spec: OAuthAuthProvider SHALL use Norn's OAuth AuthManager to acquire, cache, and refresh OAuth tokens. It reads and writes Norn-owned $NORN_HOME/auth/auth.json (default ~/.norn/auth/auth.json). The document remains Codex-compatible, but Norn does not use the Codex CLI's writable credential as its default or fallback.
acceptance: The self-contained OAuth implementation lives under provider/openai_oauth/
OAuthAuthProvider wraps Norn's AuthManager
apply_auth sets Authorization: Bearer {access_token} header
apply_auth sets ChatGPT-Account-ID header when account_id is present
Proactive token refresh: checks JWT exp before each request, refreshes if expired
Token storage: reads and writes $NORN_HOME/auth/auth.json (default ~/.norn/auth/auth.json)
If no stored token exists, returns a clear error directing the user to run login
Test: mock AuthManager returns token, verify Bearer header set correctly

requirements.title: Login flow integration
requirements.spec: Norn SHALL expose a login function that triggers its OAuth PKCE flow. This is called by the CLI/orchestrator when no stored token exists.
acceptance: pub async fn login(config: LoginConfig) -> Result<(), NornError> wraps Norn's run_login_server or device code flow
LoginConfig struct: auth_root (Option<PathBuf>), device_code (bool for headless)
On success, tokens are persisted to Norn-owned $NORN_HOME/auth/auth.json by default, using a Codex-compatible document shape
On failure, returns NornError with clear message (browser failed to open, user cancelled, etc.)
pub async fn logout(config: LoginConfig) -> Result<(), NornError> revokes tokens and clears storage
Test: verify LoginConfig construction with defaults

requirements.title: Wire auth into OpenAI provider request pipeline
requirements.spec: The OpenAI provider SHALL use AuthProvider to authenticate every HTTP request instead of directly setting a Bearer token from an API key.
acceptance: OpenAiProvider stores Arc<dyn AuthProvider> instead of SecretString api_key
build_from_auth_source(auth_source) -> Arc<dyn AuthProvider> factory function
Every reqwest request in SenderProvider::execute passes through auth_provider.apply_auth()
401 responses trigger auth_provider.on_unauthorized() for reactive refresh
SecretString api_key construction path still compiles (for ApiKey auth source) but is documented as testing-only
All existing provider tests updated to use a MockAuthProvider
Test: provider request includes Bearer header from auth provider

requirements.title: 401 recovery with token refresh
requirements.spec: When the OpenAI provider receives a 401 response, it SHALL attempt token refresh via the AuthProvider before failing.
acceptance: AuthProvider trait gains: async fn on_unauthorized(&self) -> Result<bool, ProviderError> — returns true if token was refreshed
OAuthAuthProvider::on_unauthorized triggers Norn's OAuth refresh flow
On successful refresh, the original request is retried once with the new token
On failed refresh (expired refresh token, revoked), returns ProviderError::AuthenticationFailed with message directing user to re-login
ApiKeyAuthProvider::on_unauthorized always returns false (no refresh possible)
Test: mock provider returns 401, auth provider refreshes, retry succeeds

boundaries: SHALL keep token-authority endpoints fixed to the supported Codex-compatible OAuth flow
SHALL NOT use the Codex CLI's writable credential store as Norn's default or fallback
SHALL NOT store API keys in auth.json — OAuth tokens only
SHALL NOT remove the API key code path entirely (needed for testing) but SHALL NOT expose it as a default or recommended configuration
SHALL NOT implement the device code flow in this brief — login via browser PKCE only (device code can be a follow-up)
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all tests pass (provider tests updated for MockAuthProvider)
ProviderConfig default construction uses AuthSource::OAuth, not ApiKey
grep -rn 'api_key' crates/norn/src/provider/ shows no usage outside the ApiKey variant and test helpers
401 → refresh → retry cycle verified by mock test
